repo_prior_art.cite: pid_dose_response_iv/v1: finite sharp coupling projection; pid_ordinal_late_partial_defiers/v1: finite response-path sharpness

published_prior_art.cite: MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 and HanYang2024: finite LP bounds for MTE/PRTE weighted averages; MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024: multiple-IV MTE aggregation
MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 + HanYang2024 give LP/bounding machinery; MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024 gives multi-IV PM aggregation, but no explicit zero-residual span criterion
Goff2024 point-identifies vector-monotone LATE-type parameters but does not compare them to PRTE Bernstein span residuals

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The finite LP/support-function statement is already standard LP machinery in MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018 and HanYang2024, with multiple-IV aggregation supplied by MogstadTorgovitskyWalters2024.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship: as written the kernel is a row-space diagnostic implicit in existing LP frameworks; flagship repair needs a strict new bound, open-regime theorem, or sharp frontier versus named estimators.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The §8 claim that this would unblock an MST Section 6 multi-instrument future-work question is not supported by MogstadSantosTorgovitsky2018, whose Section 6 is a concluding empirical-settings discussion and not a multi-IV open problem.

soundness_flags.label: Setup / Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The locked setup imposes one scalar latent index V with D_z=1{V<=p_z}, which is the full threshold-crossing/IAM convention, while the proposal claims the MTW partial-monotonicity regime with multiple instrument-specific threshold equations.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The equivalence r_star(P)=0 iff zero remaining variation ignores identification coming from the Bernstein shape polytope; a_star may be outside row(A) while the feasible face is flat in that direction.

soundness_flags.label: Homogeneous MTE check
soundness_flags.one_line: Restricting beta to constant MTE curves makes all normalized weights evaluate to the same scalar, but it does not imply r_star(P)=0 in R^{K+1}; the stated reduction fails under the proposal's own residual definition.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the single common V setup with the actual MTW partial-monotonicity/mutual-consistency objects, or explicitly admit the proposal is in the stronger full-threshold regime and drop the PM novelty claim.
Redefine support equivalence modulo the affine hull/normal cone of the Bernstein shape polytope, so zero width cannot be confused with row-span identification from observed chords.
Repair the homogeneous-MTE corollary by saying width collapses on the constant-MTE feasible subspace, not that the Euclidean residual r_star is zero.

To meet the flagship target, recast the main result as a sharp if-and-only-if frontier between MTW multi-IV MTE policy evaluation and Goff vector-monotone LATE identification, with an observable threshold not already implied by solving the existing LP.

recommended_next_step: Revise the setup and Conjecture 1 residual criterion before another novelty pass; the present version is promising but not flagship-clean.
